title: senecio glastifolius
intro: Senecio glastifolius, also known as Jerusalem sage, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray leaves and clusters of small, blue or purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other compounds. The health benefits of Jerusalem sage include its potential to protect against liver damage, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. Jerusalem sage can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to support liver health and alleviate symptoms associated with digestive issues and respiratory problems.
